assets.description: Automated audit of homepage messaging, clarity, trust signals, and conversion elements. Returns specific findings with before/after recommendations. Requires website URL input.

usage_rules.when_to_use: First touch with cold/paid traffic prospects. Best when you've observed a specific issue (generic hero, weak CTA, missing trust signals) and want proof of the problem.
usage_rules.when_not_to_use: Not appropriate as very first outreach to agency decision-makers (too generic). Don't use after deal is closed or with warm/hot leads who want solution, not audit.
usage_rules.conversion_rate_context: Analyser links in cold emails convert at ~8-12% to reply. Summary format performs better than full link for highest-friction ICPs.

assets.description: Interactive tool that guides users through creating a tighter, more specific creative brief. Outputs a structured 3-5 field brief that reduces revision cycles.

usage_rules.when_to_use: After positive reply or warm introduction. Use when prospect has agreed to explore solutions. Shows immediate value by improving their current process.
usage_rules.when_not_to_use: Don't use in cold first touch. Not appropriate for agencies that don't see process improvement as leverage (rare but happens).
usage_rules.conversion_rate_context: Brief Builder in follow-up emails converts at ~15-20% to time-investment. Strong engagement metric for warm leads.

assets.description: Quantifies business impact of design bottlenecks. Inputs: current production time, number of projects/campaigns, hourly team cost. Outputs: annual cost of inefficiency, potential revenue unlock.

usage_rules.when_to_use: Use when you want to quantify business impact. Strong for agencies (margin calculator focus) and marketing managers (revenue loss focus). After brief or in second/third touch.
usage_rules.when_not_to_use: Don't use if prospect hasn't agreed to explore. Founders with early-stage SaaS sometimes skip financial tools—use checklist instead.
usage_rules.conversion_rate_context: Calculator links in warm emails convert at ~20-25% to time-investment. Highest engagement among all assets. Average user spends 8-12 minutes on tool.

assets.description: 6-16 item diagnostic checklist across messaging, trust, clarity, CTA, mobile, conversion path. Self-assessment format. Prospect checks off current state, sees gap vs. best practice.

usage_rules.when_to_use: Cold first touch or second touch after analyser. Self-assessment format doesn't require prospect to open a tool. Works well for time-constrained prospects (founders, agency owners).
usage_rules.when_not_to_use: Don't use as final asset before booking call (too diagnostic, not solutionary). Marketing managers sometimes find checklists too basic—use calculator instead.
usage_rules.conversion_rate_context: Checklist PDFs in cold emails get ~25-30% open rate. Low click-through but high completion rate (60%+ of opens). Good awareness-building asset.

assets.description: Customer success story showing before/after metrics. Format: Company profile → Problem → Solution → Results (time saved, conversion improved, margin gained). 1-2 page PDF.

usage_rules.when_to_use: Use for warm leads or positive replies. Best when prospect matches customer profile (same ICP, similar business size, same pain). Always choose case study that mirrors prospect's situation.
usage_rules.when_not_to_use: Don't use in cold first touch. Don't use if prospect's situation doesn't align with case study (wrong company size, wrong pain, wrong outcome).
usage_rules.conversion_rate_context: Case studies in warm follow-ups convert at ~18-25% to next step (call, demo). Credibility multiplier when matched to prospect situation.

assets.description: Snapshot of Design Bees' operational playbook. Example: '5-Step Brief Structure for Faster Approvals' or 'How We Reduce Revision Cycles.' 2-3 pages, actionable, not salesy.

usage_rules.when_to_use: Use for agencies and marketing managers with process bottleneck pain. Shows methodology without asking for commitment. Good for thought leadership positioning.
usage_rules.when_not_to_use: Don't use for founders who don't care about detailed processes. Don't use as cold first touch (too much assumption of interest). Not appropriate for tire-kickers.
usage_rules.conversion_rate_context: Playbook excerpts get high engagement (time spent) but moderate conversion to next step (10-15%). Strong for nurture and thought leadership.

cold.logic: Use lightweight assets (checklist or analyser summary) that don't require prospect buy-in. Avoid tools that require interaction for value.

positive_reply.logic: Prospect has shown interest. Use tool that provides immediate utility (brief builder reduces their revision cycles, calculator quantifies impact). Hold solution details.

warm.logic: Prospect is actively engaged. Use high-credibility assets (calculator for ROI, case study for social proof). Lead toward call/demo.

post_call.logic: Based on call outcomes: If they want operational improvement, follow with brief_builder. If they want financial justification, follow with calculator. If they want proof, follow with case_study.

stalled.logic: Use a smaller, more specific asset tied to the exact objection or question. Avoid broad assets. Example: If stalled on time-to-implementation, use brief_builder to show how faster briefing works. If stalled on ROI, use calculator.

opportunity_reframe.logic: If prospect is not responding or seems to have lost interest, an analyser can re-engage by identifying a new issue they weren't aware of. Use as re-engagement tactic.

recommendation_engine.logic: Match by icp_fit AND issue_fit first. Filter by funnel_stage_best to ensure readiness. Exclude already-used assets to maintain novelty. Prioritize assets with highest conversion rate for this segment. Break ties by engagement level (cold → checklist, warm → calculator).

override_rules.if_multiple_issues_detected: Use analyser first to holistically identify all issues
override_rules.if_strong_financial_focus: Prioritize calculator regardless of funnel stage
override_rules.if_process_focus: Prioritize brief_builder if founder or marketing manager
override_rules.if_credibility_question: Prioritize case_study that matches prospect profile
override_rules.if_objection_handling: Use asset that directly addresses objection type (ROI objection → calculator, proof objection → case_study)

founder.rationale: Founders are time-constrained and skeptical of tools. Start lightweight (checklist), use analyser as proof of problems, calculator for impact, case study for validation.

marketing_manager.rationale: Marketing managers want proof of problems and financial impact. Analyser shows specific issues, calculator shows revenue impact, brief_builder shows process improvement, case study shows peer validation.

agency.rationale: Agencies care about margin and efficiency first. Calculator shows revenue opportunity, brief_builder shows process improvement, playbook shows methodology, case study shows results for clients.
